about.title: From idea to working product
about.description: I focus on building modern web applications using React, TypeScript, and Next.js, with strong attention to code quality, performance, and usability. I specialize in simplifying complex problems and turning them into intuitive user interfaces. I work closely with designers and stakeholders to ensure that delivered features are consistent, thoughtful, and truly useful.
highlights: In team environments, I focus on clear communication, solid organization, and continuous improvements that make collaboration smoother and more predictable.

projects.easterEggModalBody: It is, of course, a UFO abducting a cow. Not just any UFO either, but one that clearly decided this portfolio needed one more dramatic subplot. The cow did not approve this roadmap item, but the cosmos rarely waits for stakeholder sign-off.

items.title: FUNKOLOGI
items.description: A full redesign and implementation for a psychologist's website, focused on trust, clearer positioning, accessibility, SEO, and a setup that supports easier self-managed content updates.
paragraphs: The starting point was straightforward: the client had previously built her own WordPress site, but the result did not communicate professionalism or trust. On top of that, she needed a setup that would let her add materials herself instead of relying on a developer for every update.

I approached the work as a mix of redesign, information architecture cleanup, and technical groundwork for future content growth. Beyond the visual layer, a key part of the delivery was building a concrete content flow: a public materials library with search, an admin area, authentication, and a form for uploading PDFs with metadata.

The final result was a modern Next.js website with stronger branding, clearer messaging, a structured materials library, and a working Firebase-backed admin flow. It was also built with accessibility, SEO, and long-term maintainability in mind. The key business outcome was simple: the client now has a site that looks credible and supports self-managed publishing and organizing of new resources.
bulletPoints: Moved the brand away from a cluttered self-built WordPress site into a polished custom frontend experience.
Designed a calm and trustworthy visual identity for families seeking specialist psychological support.
Simplified the content structure so visitors can quickly understand the offer, specialization, and next steps.
Designed a materials library with search, popular queries, and clear category cards.
Built an admin area with login, a published resources list, and edit/delete actions.
Added a PDF publishing flow with metadata, keywords, and file upload handling.
Connected Firebase Authentication, Storage, and Firestore as the content management backbone.
Built the project with accessibility, SEO, future growth, and scalable content operations in mind.

items.title: Northstar Commerce
items.description: A product-style e-commerce operations dashboard for monitoring revenue, conversion, order flow, acquisition performance, customer retention, and operational risk in one workspace.
paragraphs: Northstar Commerce was shaped as a focused dashboard for an e-commerce operations manager rather than a generic analytics demo. The goal was to make the product story clearer: who the workspace is for, which daily decisions it supports, and why each view exists.

The product workflow moves from top-line storefront health into operational follow-up. The overview helps users review revenue, orders, average order value, conversion rate, retention, refund signals, channel performance, category mix, loyalty tiers, and order risk. The Order Intelligence view then turns that monitoring layer into a searchable record-level workspace with saved views, filters, quick presets, summary cards, a data table, and a watchlist for active issues.

Supporting Account and Preferences screens were treated as real product surfaces instead of placeholders. The final experience uses a restrained data palette, clearer chart hierarchy, product-oriented copy, light and dark theme readability improvements, and responsive summaries that keep dense charts and full grids reserved for larger screens.
bulletPoints: Designed the project around a specific primary user: an e-commerce operations manager.
Built an overview dashboard for revenue, orders, average order value, conversion, retention, refunds, channel performance, category mix, and order risk.
Created an Order Intelligence workspace with saved views, explicit filters, search, quick presets, summary cards, an operational table, and watchlist notes.
Reframed Account and Preferences as supporting product screens for workspace identity, reporting preferences, alerts, digest emails, visible metrics, and shared dashboards.
Used product-oriented e-commerce terminology so the dashboard feels like an operations tool rather than a template demo.
Refined the visual system around navy, teal, sage, amber, and coral with improved chart readability in light and dark themes.
Kept the implementation portfolio-scoped with demo data and preview-only actions while preserving a clear product narrative.
Delivered the project with a clean production build and lint pass.

meta.label: Stack
meta.value: Next.js, React, TypeScript, MUI, Chart.js, Sass modules, NextAuth

slides.title: Overview Dashboard
slides.caption: The main view focuses on daily storefront monitoring through KPI cards, revenue trends, retention and refund signals, acquisition channels, customer mix, category demand, and loyalty indicators.

slides.title: Order Intelligence
slides.caption: The analytics workspace extends the dashboard into record-level investigation with saved views, filter controls, quick presets, searchable orders, risk exposure summaries, and a watchlist for follow-up.

slides.title: Account and Preferences
slides.caption: The supporting screens cover workspace identity, reporting preferences, visible metrics, alert settings, digest emails, and shared dashboards so the product feels complete beyond the analytics views.
